claims.summary: A half-open or idle WebSocket can linger because the subscriber has no payload-idle timeout.
claims.disposition: Verified the receive loop waits on WebSocket input or shutdown and has no idle reconnect timer; this is a real architectural gap, not a dismissed report.

claims.summary: Connection establishment must be distinguished from first valid payload and continuing freshness.
claims.disposition: Verified current connect, first-valid lag, cadence, latest-valid age, parse-error, and stale-content evidence remain separate.

claims.summary: A half-open fixture should measure connect-to-first-valid and valid-payload idle behavior.
claims.disposition: Routed a bounded fake-socket observation that can classify idle/stale behavior without touching the live subscriber.

claims.summary: The subscriber should enforce a maximum idle period and reconnect.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because an idle timeout changes live reconnect cadence, telemetry admission, and runtime observation continuity.
